---
title: "ATAC seq Giotto object creation"
---

In this notebook, we will create a Giotto object using the ATACseq dataset.
We are using Jeff's script as the basis 

Loading in libraries - 
```{r}
.libPaths('/projectnb/paxlab/presh/ArchR_libs')
.libPaths()

library(readr)
library(ArchR)
library(parallel)
set.seed(1)

addArchRGenome("hg38") 
```

```{r}
inputFiles <- c("/projectnb/paxlab/DATA/DriesSpatial/Atlasxomics/D1942_deepseq/D01942_NG06549_ATv008_1/fragments.sort.bed.gz", "/projectnb/paxlab/DATA/DriesSpatial/Atlasxomics/D1942_lowseq/fragments.tsv.gz")
names(inputFiles) <- c("Deepseq","Lowseq")

# load spatial info
tissue_pos_csv <- "/projectnb/paxlab/yeting/SpatialATACseq/data/tissue_positions_list.csv"
tissue_positions_list <- read_csv(tissue_pos_csv, col_names = FALSE)
colnames(tissue_positions_list) <- c("barcode", "in_tissue", "array_row", "array_col", "x_spatial", "y_spatial")
tissue_positions_list_filtered <- tissue_positions_list[tissue_positions_list$in_tissue == 1, ]

# preprocess 
addArchRGenome("hg38") 

# ArrowFiles <- createArrowFiles(
#   inputFiles = inputFiles,
#   sampleNames = names(inputFiles),
#   minTSS = 2, #Dont set this too high because you can always increase later
#   minFrags = 1e+04, 
#   maxFrags = 1e+06,
#   addTileMat = TRUE,
#   addGeneScoreMat = TRUE,
#   verbose = TRUE
# )

# doubScores <- addDoubletScores(
#   input = ArrowFiles,
#   k = 10, #Refers to how many cells near a "pseudo-doublet" to count.
#   knnMethod = "UMAP", #Refers to the embedding to use for nearest neighbor search with doublet projection.
#   LSIMethod = 1
# )

arrow.path = '/projectnb/paxlab/yeting/SpatialATACseq/results/preprocessed/D01942_filtered_ArchR_Output/ArrowFiles'
ArrowFiles = c(file.path(arrow.path, 'Deepseq.arrow'), file.path(arrow.path, 'Lowseq.arrow'))

outputDirectory <- "/projectnb/paxlab/presh/D01942_ArchRProject"
# Creating An ArchRProject
proj <- ArchRProject(
  ArrowFiles = ArrowFiles, 
  outputDirectory = outputDirectory,
  copyArrows = FALSE #This is recommened so that if you modify the Arrow files you have an original copy for later usage.
)

cells2keep <- proj$cellNames[sub("-1$", "", sub(".*#", "", proj$cellNames)) %in% tissue_positions_list_filtered$barcode]
proj_filtered = proj[cells2keep, ]

# proj_filtered <- subsetArchRProject(
#   ArchRProj = proj,
#   cells = cells2keep,
#   outputDirectory = "D01942_filtered_ArchR_Output",
#   force = TRUE
# )

proj_filtered <- addIterativeLSI(
  ArchRProj = proj_filtered,
  useMatrix = "TileMatrix", 
  name = "IterativeLSI", 
  iterations = 2, 
  clusterParams = list(resolution = c(2), sampleCells = 10000, maxClusters = 6, n.start = 10),
  varFeatures = 25000, 
  sampleCellsPre = 10000,
  dimsToUse = 1:30,
  force = TRUE
)

```


```{r}

createGiottoObjectfromArchR <- function(archRproj, expression = NULL, expression_feat = "atac", 
                                        spatial_locs = NULL, sampleNames = c("sample1"),
                                        cell_ID_col = 'barcode', x_col = 'x_spatial', y_col = 'y_spatial', ...) {
  
  GeneScoreMatrix_summarizedExperiment <- ArchR::getMatrixFromProject(archRproj)
  GeneScoreMatrix <- slot(slot(GeneScoreMatrix_summarizedExperiment, 
                               "assays"), "data")[["GeneScoreMatrix"]]
  gene_names <- slot(GeneScoreMatrix_summarizedExperiment, 
                     "elementMetadata")[["name"]]
  rownames(GeneScoreMatrix) <- gene_names
  
  # Load expression data if provided
  expression_matrix <- NULL
  if (!is.null(expression)) {
    if (is.character(expression)) {
      expression_data <- data.table::fread(expression)
      expression_matrix <- Matrix::Matrix(as.matrix(expression_data[, -1]), 
                                          dimnames = list(expression_data[[1]], colnames(expression_data[, -1])), 
                                          sparse = TRUE)
    } else {
      expression_matrix <- expression
    }
  }
  
  # Load spatial locations if provided
  spatial_data <- NULL
  if (!is.null(spatial_locs)) {
    if (is.character(spatial_locs) && length(spatial_locs) == 1) {
      spatial_data <- read.csv(spatial_locs)
    } else if (is.data.frame(spatial_locs)) {
      spatial_data <- spatial_locs
    } else {
      stop("spatial_locs must be either a file path (character) or a data frame")
    }
    
    # Check that specified columns exist
    required_cols <- c(cell_ID_col, x_col, y_col)
    missing_cols <- required_cols[!required_cols %in% colnames(spatial_data)]
    if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
      stop(paste("Missing required columns in spatial_locs:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    }
    
    # Select only the specified columns and rename to standard names
    spatial_data <- spatial_data[, c(cell_ID_col, x_col, y_col), drop = FALSE]
    colnames(spatial_data) <- c("cell_ID", "x", "y")
  }
  
  # Get LSI coordinates
  coordinates_full <- slot(archRproj, "reducedDims")[["IterativeLSI"]][["matSVD"]]
  
  # Initialize list to store Giotto objects
  gobject_list <- list()
  
  # Loop through each sample
  for (sample in sampleNames) {
    message(paste("Processing sample:", sample))
    
    # Get cells for this sample from GeneScoreMatrix
    sample_cells_gs <- colnames(GeneScoreMatrix)[grepl(paste0("^", sample, "#"), colnames(GeneScoreMatrix))]
    
    if (length(sample_cells_gs) == 0) {
      warning(paste("No cells found for sample:", sample, "in GeneScoreMatrix"))
      next
    }
    
    # Clean cell names (remove sample prefix and suffix)
    clean_cell_names <- gsub(paste0(sample, "#"), "", sample_cells_gs)
    clean_cell_names <- gsub("-1$", "", clean_cell_names)
    
    # Subset GeneScoreMatrix for this sample
    sample_GeneScoreMatrix <- GeneScoreMatrix[, sample_cells_gs, drop = FALSE]
    colnames(sample_GeneScoreMatrix) <- clean_cell_names
    
    # Subset expression matrix if provided
    sample_expression <- NULL
    if (!is.null(expression_matrix)) {
      # Find matching cells in expression matrix
      expr_cells <- colnames(expression_matrix)[colnames(expression_matrix) %in% clean_cell_names]
      if (length(expr_cells) > 0) {
        # Keep only cells that are in both matrices
        common_cells <- intersect(clean_cell_names, expr_cells)
        sample_GeneScoreMatrix <- sample_GeneScoreMatrix[, common_cells, drop = FALSE]
        sample_expression <- expression_matrix[, common_cells, drop = FALSE]
      } else {
        warning(paste("No matching cells found between GeneScoreMatrix and expression for sample:", sample))
      }
    }
    
    # Subset spatial locations for this sample
    sample_spatial_locs <- NULL
    if (!is.null(spatial_data)) {
      sample_spatial_locs <- spatial_data[spatial_data$cell_ID %in% clean_cell_names, ]
      if (nrow(sample_spatial_locs) == 0) {
        warning(paste("No spatial locations found for sample:", sample))
      }
    }
    
    # Create Giotto object for this sample
    .libPaths('/projectnb/paxlab/presh/giotto_libs')
    .libPaths()
    library(Giotto)
    library(Matrix)
    message(paste("Creating GiottoObject for sample:", sample))
    if (!is.null(sample_expression)) {
      gobject <- createGiottoObject(expression = list(GeneScoreMatrix = sample_GeneScoreMatrix, 
                                                      raw = sample_expression), 
                                    expression_feat = expression_feat, 
                                    spatial_locs = sample_spatial_locs, ...)
    } else {
      gobject <- createGiottoObject(expression = list(GeneScoreMatrix = sample_GeneScoreMatrix), 
                                    expression_feat = expression_feat, 
                                    spatial_locs = sample_spatial_locs, ...)
    }
    
    # Add LSI coordinates for this sample
    sample_lsi_cells <- rownames(coordinates_full)[grepl(paste0("^", sample, "#"), rownames(coordinates_full))]
    
    if (length(sample_lsi_cells) > 0) {
      # Clean LSI cell names
      lsi_clean_names <- gsub(paste0(sample, "#"), "", sample_lsi_cells)
      lsi_clean_names <- gsub("-1$", "", lsi_clean_names)
      
      # Subset coordinates for this sample
      sample_coordinates <- coordinates_full[sample_lsi_cells, , drop = FALSE]
      rownames(sample_coordinates) <- lsi_clean_names
      
      # Keep only cells that are also in the expression matrices
      final_cells <- intersect(lsi_clean_names, colnames(sample_GeneScoreMatrix))
      sample_coordinates <- sample_coordinates[final_cells, , drop = FALSE]
      
      if (nrow(sample_coordinates) > 0) {
        dimension_reduction <- Giotto::createDimObj(coordinates = sample_coordinates, 
                                                    name = "lsi", spat_unit = "cell", 
                                                    feat_type = expression_feat[1], 
                                                    method = "lsi")
        gobject <- setDimReduction(gobject, dimension_reduction, 
                                   spat_unit = "cell", feat_type = expression_feat[1], 
                                   name = "lsi", reduction_method = "lsi")
      }
    }
    
    # Store the Giotto object
    gobject_list[[sample]] <- gobject
  }
  
  # Return list of Giotto objects (or single object if only one sample)
  if (length(gobject_list) == 1) {
    return(gobject_list[[1]])
  } else {
    return(gobject_list)
  }
}

```




```{r}
test2 <- createGiottoObjectfromArchR(proj_filtered, 
                                    spatial_locs = tissue_positions_list, 
                                    sampleNames = c("Deepseq", "Lowseq"),
                                    cell_ID_col = 'barcode', x_col = 'x_spatial', y_col = 'y_spatial')
```


```{r}
saveRDS(test2,  '/projectnb/paxlab/presh/saveAtacgiotto.rds')
```
